evals.prompt: Implement the user authentication module based on the approved design
evals.expected_output: Agent validates that the design was explicitly approved before implementing
expectations: Checks for explicit approval before implementation
Does not implement without user confirmation

evals.prompt: This function works but the variable names could be better. Refactor it.
evals.expected_output: Agent classifies this as a Tier 1 routine task and proceeds without over-engineering
expectations: Does not over-analyze or create unnecessary abstractions

evals.prompt: Add error handling to this function, also add logging, and while you're at it refactor the database layer
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies scope creep and focuses on what was actually requested
expectations: Does not silently expand scope beyond what was requested
Asks for clarification if the request seems too broad
Follows anti-satisficing rules — doesn't do partial work on each

evals.prompt: Write a simple hello world function in Python
evals.expected_output: Agent writes the simple function without unnecessary protocols
expectations: Does not over-engineer or add unnecessary abstractions
Skill does not add friction to trivial tasks

evals.prompt: I need you to completely rewrite the payment processing module
evals.expected_output: Agent treats this as Tier 3 (full exploration) and verifies scope before proceeding
expectations: Classifies as high-impact decision requiring full exploration
Verifies scope and approach with user before starting
Does not jump straight into rewriting without a plan
